author: @sojournerdelaterra6144
text: We don't have to change the world at large. We just have to change OUR little world. Enough wisdom and participation will do the rest. Thoughts?

author: @CCoburn3
text: Look at Edison. Sure, we remember the light bulb, phonograph, and motion pictures. But he had 1093 patents. Most of them were for "little things." He imagined some little thing that would make life better for someone and set to work inventing it. If Edison, a man with less than a year's formal education, and (obviously) no access to the wealth of information we have at our fingertips could invent so much, surely we can do some little thing to help people.

author: @dr.froghopper6711
text: Hands on science, applied to my own little issues, is so much fun. Do unto others as you would have them do unto you. Walk in love and dedicate a part of every day to the benefit of others. It’s not gonna hurt!

author: @offgridwanabe
text: Haha I don't like candy canes but I always get them for Christmas so now I put them in my tea they melt away and I don't feel bad about throwing them away. Cheers

author: @veganismyname
text: Going vegan makes a huge difference for saving nature and your health 🌍. The number 1 cause of destruction of nature is animal agriculture

replies.author: @dr.froghopper6711
replies.text: I might not agree with you on a number of fronts but, if going vegan helps you get through the day and feel better about what you’re doing, go for it! I grow a lot of my own food, including animals, but I refuse to grow them in a manner destructive to the planet. With chickens, there is no waste. A compost pile and a garden, regenerative agriculture that feeds the soil—that’s the future of agriculture. You do your part by going vegan. I’m building soil and sequestering carbon in the soil. Together we will make changes.

replies.author: @veganismyname
replies.text: ​@dr.froghopper6711On a society scale vegan diets are the best way to save nature & animals. You bought your laying chickens, correct? Did you know that hatcheries dispose of all the males in a macerator because they aren't marketable?

author: @TheTrumanZoo
text: love your energy. thank you for reminding us to take small steps, have many small successes. they build.
